title: Israel’s Buffer-Zone Fallacy
content: In an era when ballistic missiles, drones, and other projectiles can travel long distances to hit targets with growing accuracy, the idea of a protective buffer zone is not only faulty; it is complete nonsense. Yet this is precisely the rationale that Israel has offered for its occupation of southern Lebanon.
snippet: In an era when ballistic missiles, drones, and other projectiles can travel long distances to hit targets with growing accuracy, the idea of a protective buffer zone is not only faulty; it is complete
creator: Daoud Kuttab
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: How Women Succeed in Male-Dominated Fields
content: Discussions about gender and work often morph into cultural clashes, driven by fear, ideology, or frustration. But recent empirical research suggests that having female peers and professors can help women succeed in the workplace without hurting men.
snippet: Discussions about gender and work often morph into cultural clashes, driven by fear, ideology, or frustration. But recent empirical research suggests that having female peers and professors can help w
creator: Fernanda Estevan
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Real AI Race
content: While the invention of general-purpose technologies creates new opportunities, it is their diffusion across industries and economies that drives economic transformation. Early evidence suggests that AI is likely to follow a similar trajectory.
snippet: While the invention of general-purpose technologies creates new opportunities, it is their diffusion across industries and economies that drives economic transformation. Early evidence suggests that A
creator: Lee Jong-Wha
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Is Economic Forecasting Still Possible?
content: Much of the forecasting profession—and the economic theory that underpins it—still defaults to single baselines that treat the future as a probabilistic replica of the past. But when the structure of the economy changes in unforeseeable ways, as it is now, forecasters must acknowledge that many futures are possible.
snippet: Much of the forecasting profession—and the economic theory that underpins it—still defaults to single baselines that treat the future as a probabilistic replica of the past. But when the structure of
creator: Morten Nyboe Tabor
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Can India Become the World’s Innovation Capital?
content: As supply chains fragment, AI rewrites the rules of competition, and geopolitical volatility intensifies, companies are searching for stable, scalable, innovation‑rich environments. India can provide what they need, and with the right enabling policies, it can transform its advantages into a lasting global lead.
snippet: As supply chains fragment, AI rewrites the rules of competition, and geopolitical volatility intensifies, companies are searching for stable, scalable, innovation‑rich environments. India can provide
creator: Shashi Tharoor
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: A Bad Deal Today Means a Bigger War Tomorrow
content: Iran has given the international community little reason to trust it since the 1979 revolution that led to the establishment of the Islamic Republic. Negotiations to end the war today would be dangerously premature, naive, and likely to produce an outcome that all but guarantees another war.
snippet: Iran has given the international community little reason to trust it since the 1979 revolution that led to the establishment of the Islamic Republic. Negotiations to end the war today would be dangero
creator: Mohammed Al Dhaheri
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: America’s Currency Is the Global South’s Problem
content: While the US dollar will not retain its preeminent status forever, countries in the Global South cannot afford to wait out its dominance. With a strategic approach, they can reduce their dependence on the dollar, thereby mitigating the associated risks and fostering a more balanced and resilient global financial landscape.
snippet: While the US dollar will not retain its preeminent status forever, countries in the Global South cannot afford to wait out its dominance. With a strategic approach, they can reduce their dependence on
creator: Janak Raj
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Bombing for Freedom
content: The idea that bombing civilians will create the conditions for “regime change” is more than a century old. It has never worked, and it won’t work in Iran, because when people are expending all their energy trying to make it to the next day, they don’t have any left over for rebellion.
snippet: The idea that bombing civilians will create the conditions for “regime change” is more than a century old. It has never worked, and it won’t work in Iran, because when people are expending all their e
creator: Ian Buruma
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Tragedy Is Reborn as Hope
content: As the world confronts the twin crises of democratic backsliding and climate change, a new sense of possibility has emerged from tragedy. US President Donald Trump has shown that the person holding political power does, in fact, matter, while the senseless war in the Middle East has renewed hope for completing the energy transition.
snippet: As the world confronts the twin crises of democratic backsliding and climate change, a new sense of possibility has emerged from tragedy. US President Donald Trump has shown that the person holding po
creator: Chiara Cordelli
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Iran on the Edge of Breakdown
content: The sustained US-Israeli attacks on Iranian infrastructure have left the country’s legal system without functional enforcement mechanisms. Regardless of whether the Islamic Republic collapses or endures, the country is likely to face a prolonged period of instability.
snippet: The sustained US-Israeli attacks on Iranian infrastructure have left the country’s legal system without functional enforcement mechanisms. Regardless of whether the Islamic Republic collapses or endur
creator: Pegah Banihashemi
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Why Iran Is Beating America
content: The “asymmetric cost” model—a war the US starts will ultimately cost the other side far more—has proven vital to sustain the illusion of American invincibility and to limit domestic political resistance to US military adventurism. Now, Iran has broken it.
snippet: The “asymmetric cost” model—a war the US starts will ultimately cost the other side far more—has proven vital to sustain the illusion of American invincibility and to limit domestic political resistan
creator: Brahma Chellaney
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Is Financial Deregulation Under Trump Going Too Far?
content: Although the Trump administration is rightly rethinking the heavy-handed banking measures implemented after the 2008 financial crisis, it is also slashing staff at key regulatory agencies, reducing capital requirements, and pushing through other changes. No matter how you slice it, the odds of a bad ending have risen.
snippet: Although the Trump administration is rightly rethinking the heavy-handed banking measures implemented after the 2008 financial crisis, it is also slashing staff at key regulatory agencies, reducing ca
creator: Kenneth Rogoff
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: When Fools Go to War
content: One need only look past the moral and strategic differences between Iran and Ukraine to see that both are facing similar situations. Both have been attacked by larger powers whose institutional decline has produced regimes that failed to anticipate what they were setting into motion.
snippet: One need only look past the moral and strategic differences between Iran and Ukraine to see that both are facing similar situations. Both have been attacked by larger powers whose institutional declin
creator: Federico Fubini
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Economic Impact of Mexico’s Autocratic Drift
content: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum suffered a rare legislative setback in March, one of several early signs that support for her and her predecessor’s political project may be softening. The narrative that Mexico could bend its democratic rules without weakening its economic foundations is now beginning to unravel.
snippet: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum suffered a rare legislative setback in March, one of several early signs that support for her and her predecessor’s political project may be softening. The narrativ
creator: Guillermo Ortiz
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: African Clean-Energy Sovereignty Can’t Wait
content: Renewable energy is the only way that African economies can escape permanent dependence on foreign imported fuels, currencies, external creditors, and the disastrous effects of wars in other parts of the world. More than a climate measure, it is the foundation for sovereignty and long-term economic stability.
snippet: Renewable energy is the only way that African economies can escape permanent dependence on foreign imported fuels, currencies, external creditors, and the disastrous effects of wars in other parts of
creator: Fadhel Kaboub
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Big Tech Shouldn’t Be Writing the Rules for AI
content: Anthropic’s clash with the Trump administration has exposed the extent to which governments have abdicated their responsibility over AI governance. To prevent powerful technologies from being shaped solely by corporate profit incentives, democracies must establish the institutions needed to oversee them.
snippet: Anthropic’s clash with the Trump administration has exposed the extent to which governments have abdicated their responsibility over AI governance. To prevent powerful technologies from being shaped s
creator: Gabriela Ramos
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Telling the Truth About China’s Success
content: With the Persian Gulf in flames, de-escalation of the cold war between the United States and China must become the world’s top priority. To that end, it is essential to explode a powerful myth: the idea that China has cheated its way to prosperity.
snippet: With the Persian Gulf in flames, de-escalation of the cold war between the United States and China must become the world’s top priority. To that end, it is essential to explode a powerful myth: the id
creator: Yanis Varoufakis
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Why US-China Decoupling Isn’t Happening
content: Despite ongoing debates about Sino-American decoupling, the global economy remains as integrated as ever. Whenever either superpower tries to restrict ties with the other, it accelerates a broader process of adaptation and adjustment that is making the system more resilient and harder to control unilaterally.
snippet: Despite ongoing debates about Sino-American decoupling, the global economy remains as integrated as ever. Whenever either superpower tries to restrict ties with the other, it accelerates a broader pro
creator: Robin Hu
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Why Europe Is Unlikely to Face an Inflation Surge
content: In 2021-22, the European Central Bank failed to pay sufficient heed to mounting inflationary pressures, leading to a delayed response that made matters much worse. While today’s energy-price shock remains less acute than the one caused by Russia’s 2022 invasion of Ukraine, the ECB appears committed to a more proactive response.
snippet: In 2021-22, the European Central Bank failed to pay sufficient heed to mounting inflationary pressures, leading to a delayed response that made matters much worse. While today’s energy-price shock rem
creator: Daniel Gros
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Can China Grow From Within?
content: At a time of intensifying geopolitical volatility, China’s embrace of a consumption-led model is not only about rebalancing growth, but also about anchoring it more firmly at home. Domestic demand offers insulation from external shocks, and along with developed capital markets, it can go a long way toward strengthening autonomy.
snippet: At a time of intensifying geopolitical volatility, China’s embrace of a consumption-led model is not only about rebalancing growth, but also about anchoring it more firmly at home. Domestic demand off
creator: Keyu Jin
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Coming Inflation-Deflation Whipsaw
content: Other than the pandemic-era supply-chain disruptions, business leaders and investors have not had occasion to think much about either inflation or deflation. But that needs to change, because we are now facing both an immediate threat of higher prices and a longer-term prospect of rapidly falling labor, goods, and services costs.
snippet: Other than the pandemic-era supply-chain disruptions, business leaders and investors have not had occasion to think much about either inflation or deflation. But that needs to change, because we are n
creator: Dambisa Moyo
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Road to De-Escalation With Iran
content: Around the world, cash-strapped governments and people already struggling with poverty and precarity are asking with increasing urgency: Is there an off-ramp for the latest conflict in the Middle East? There is, but it requires taking economic incentives seriously.
snippet: Around the world, cash-strapped governments and people already struggling with poverty and precarity are asking with increasing urgency: Is there an off-ramp for the latest conflict in the Middle East
creator: Simon Johnson
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Mispricing of War
content: The US-Israeli campaign against Iran is reminding everyone that war is one of the most globally subsidized of human activities. Those who initiate it rarely bear the full costs, which tend to be displaced across borders, markets, and time, making decisions to initiate hostilities more likely.
snippet: The US-Israeli campaign against Iran is reminding everyone that war is one of the most globally subsidized of human activities. Those who initiate it rarely bear the full costs, which tend to be displ
creator: Antara Haldar
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Energy Transition Has Its Own Strait of Hormuz
content: The clean-energy transition is often framed as a way to escape the strategic chokepoints and maritime vulnerabilities that have long defined the fossil-fuel industry. But a system built on renewables introduces new risks, as critical bottlenecks shift to refining, processing, and mineral-supply chains.

creator: Dianne Araral
